commands.repair_reason: - [P2] No repair lane is needed; the current PR patch is already the narrow docs fix and has no actionable review finding.; Cleared: The diff changes only a Markdown docs page and introduces no code execution, dependency, credential, workflow, or supply-chain surface. commands.review_summary:
The PR updates `docs/gateway/cli-backends.md` so the quick-start `openclaw agent` command includes `--agent main` and adds a short note about replacing `main` in multi-agent setups.

PR surface: Docs +3. Total +3 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection is enough: current docs omit a selector, and `src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts` rejects an `openclaw agent` run with no `--agent`, `--session-key`, `--session-id`, or `--to`.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed; the current PR patch is already the narrow docs fix and has no actionable review finding.

target.title: docs(cli): add agent selector to CLI backend quick start
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the CLI Backends quick-start command still omitted the session selector that `openclaw agent` requires.
- Why it matters: the documented no-config Codex CLI smoke command fails as written even though `--model codex-cli/gpt-5.5` is now supported.
- What changed: the quick-start command now passes `--agent main`, and the docs explain that `main` is the default agent id when no explicit agent list is configured.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no CLI behavior, gateway behavior, plugin backend behavior, or model defaults changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #68940
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `openclaw agent` gained the documented model override path, but this quick-start snippet still lacked one of the required session selectors.
- Missing detection / guardrail: docs did not include a selector-valid CLI Backends quick-start command.
- Contributing context (if known): current `docs/cli/agent.md` already documents that `--to`, `--session-id`, or `--agent` is required.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: N/A
- Scenario the test should lock in: N/A
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: this is a docs-only correction to an example command.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: no runtime behavior changed.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

The CLI Backends quick-start docs now show `openclaw agent --agent main --message "hi" --model codex-cli/gpt-5.5`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): CLI docs
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Read `docs/gateway/cli-backends.md` quick-start command.
2. Compare it against `docs/cli/agent.md` and current `openclaw agent` selector checks.
3. Update the quick-start command to include the default `--agent main` selector.

### Expected

- The documented command includes a selector accepted by `openclaw agent`.

### Actual

- The previous documented command omitted `--to`, `--session-id`, and `--agent`.

## Evidence

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Docs-only change; validation is listed below.

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: confirmed `openclaw agent` requires `--to`, `--session-id`, or `--agent`; confirmed no-config agent ids fall back to `main`; confirmed the CLI docs already describe the selector requirement.
- Edge cases checked: checked for an existing open PR covering #68940 / CLI Backends quick-start / agent selector before opening this PR.
- What you did **not** verify: did not run a live `openclaw agent` invocation because this PR only changes documentation.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

None.

## Local validation

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/gateway/cli-backends.md`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `$oss-pr-contributions` public preflight
target.author: vyctorbrzezowski
